testcase.name: test_save_many

failure.message: urllib3.exceptions.HTTPError: Save many failed with status <MagicMock name='request().status' id='4384243024'>

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestOpenLibrary testMethod=test_save_many> mock_post = <MagicMock name='request' id='4390195472'>
@patch('urllib3.PoolManager.request') def test_save_many(self, mock_post): edition = self.ol.Edition( edition_olid='OL123M', work_olid='OL12W', title='minimal edition' ) work = self.ol.Work(olid='OL12W', title='minimal work') > self.ol.save_many([edition, work], "test comment")
tests/test_openlibrary.py:268: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <olclient.openlibrary.OpenLibrary object at 0x105a22150> docs = [<class 'olclient.openlibrary.OpenLibrary.Edition.<locals>.Edition' {'_work': None, 'work_olid': 'OL12W', 'olid': 'OL1...k' {'identifiers': {}, 'olid': 'OL12W', '_editions': [], 'description': None, 'notes': None, 'title': 'minimal work'}>] comment = 'test comment'
def save_many(self, docs, comment) -> Any: """ Uses the Open Library save_many API endpoint to write any number or combination of documents (Edition, Work, or Author) back to Open Library. Uses HTTP Extension Framework custom headers (RFC 2774). """ headers = { 'Opt': '"http://openlibrary.org/dev/docs/api"; ns=42', '42-comment': comment, } doc_json = [doc.json() for doc in docs] response = self.http.request( 'POST', f'{self.base_url}/api/save_many', body=json.dumps(doc_json), headers=headers ) if response.status != 200: > raise HTTPError(f"Save many failed with status {response.status}") E urllib3.exceptions.HTTPError: Save many failed with status <MagicMock name='request().status' id='4384243024'>
olclient/openlibrary.py:140: HTTPError

testcase.name: test_load_by_isbn

failure.message: urllib3.exceptions.HTTPError: Get response failed with status <MagicMock name='request().status' id='4390321296'>

testcase.failure:
self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_isbn> mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4389895440'>
def test_load_by_isbn(self, mock_get): isbn_key = 'ISBN:0137903952' isbn_bibkeys = { isbn_key: { 'info_url': "https://openlibrary.org/books/%s/Artificial_intelligence" % self.target_olid } } mock_get.return_value.data.side_effect = [ json.dumps(isbn_bibkeys).encode('utf-8'), self.raw_edition.copy(), self.raw_author.copy(), ]
> actual = json.loads(jsonpickle.encode(self.ol.Edition.get(isbn='0137903952'))) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
tests/test_openlibrary.py:340: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ olclient/openlibrary.py:387: in get olid = cls.get_olid(bibkey, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:433: in get_olid metadata = cls.get_metadata(key, value) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ olclient/openlibrary.py:477: in get_metadata response = cls.OL.get_ol_response(path) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/backoff/_sync.py:105: in retry ret = target(*args, **kwargs) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <olclient.openlibrary.OpenLibrary object at 0x105c6bf90> path = '/api/books.json?bibkeys=ISBN:0137903952'
@backoff.on_exception(on_giveup=err, **BACKOFF_KWARGS) # type: ignore def get_ol_response(self, path): """Makes best effort to perform request w/ exponential backoff""" response = self.http.request('GET', self.base_url + path) if response.status != 200: > raise HTTPError(f"Get response failed with status {response.status}") E urllib3.exceptions.HTTPError: Get response failed with status <MagicMock name='request().status' id='4390321296'>
olclient/openlibrary.py:156: HTTPError

self = <tests.test_openlibrary.TestFullEditionGet testMethod=test_load_by_olid> mock_get = <MagicMock name='request' id='4391954768'>
def test_load_by_olid(self, mock_get): mock_get.return_value.data.side_effect = [ self.raw_edition.copy(), self.raw_author.copy(), ]
actual = json.loads( > jsonpickle.encode(self.ol.Edition.get(olid=self.target_olid)) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ )

self = <olclient.openlibrary.OpenLibrary object at 0x105c7c390> path = '/books/OL3702561M.json'
@backoff.on_exception(on_giveup=err, **BACKOFF_KWARGS) # type: ignore def get_ol_response(self, path): """Makes best effort to perform request w/ exponential backoff""" response = self.http.request('GET', self.base_url + path) if response.status != 200: > raise HTTPError(f"Get response failed with status {response.status}") E urllib3.exceptions.HTTPError: Get response failed with status <MagicMock name='request().status' id='4390235152'>
